question_text: Love and God (also known as "Kais Aur Laila") is a 1986 film K. Asif. The film had a long and troubled production history with shooting starting in 1963 with Guru Dutt as Kais and Nimmi as Laila. However Guru Dutt's sudden death in 1964 left the film incomplete and it was shelved. Then, Asif recast Sanjeev Kumar as Kais and resumed production of the film in 1970. Unfortunately, the director K. Asif died on 9 March 1971 at the age of 49 and the film was once again left incomplete. Fifteen years later, Asif's senior widow Akhtar Asif decided to release the incomplete film with the help of producer-director-distributor K. C. Bokadia. In a few months, they managed to salvage some usable portions of the incomplete film from three different studios and pieced them together. This cut-paste incomplete version was finally released on 27 May 1986. By the time of the film's release, several of the film's cast members had died, including its leading actor Sanjeev Kumar, who died in 1985. What unique Guinness record is held by this movie?
answer_text: Longest production time for a Bollywood movie
confirmation_text: Correct. Longest production time for a Bollywood movie, 20 something years.

discussion.text: Fewest alive cast members by the time of release?
Correct. Longest production time for a Bollywood movie, 20 something years.
